pattern: Counting equal pairs is just counting how many ways to choose 2 items from each group of equal values, which a single frequency table gives directly.
hints: A pair (i, j) with i < j and nums[i] == nums[j] only depends on how many times each value occurs, not on the positions themselves.
Tally how many times each value appears in the array using a frequency map.
For a value that appears c times, it contributes c*(c-1)/2 good pairs; sum that over every distinct value.

edge_inputs.description: mixed values with a repeated pair and a repeated triple
all distinct values, no pairs at all
single element can never form a pair
one repeated value buried among many distinct ones

stress.description: random arrays over the small value range so duplicates are common
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
